content:
# Joshua Chapter 23 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 247-248 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_023_page_001.jpg`

1. And it was after many days, when YHWH had given rest to Israel from all their enemies round about, and Joshua had grown old, advanced in days,

2. that Joshua summoned all Israel — their elders, and their heads, and their judges, and their officers — and said to them: "I have grown old, I am advanced in days.

3. And you have seen all that YHWH your God has done to all these nations before you, for YHWH your God is the one who fought for you.

4. See, I have allotted to you these remaining nations as an inheritance for your tribes, from the Jordan, and all the nations that I have cut off, to the Great Sea toward the setting of the sun.

5. And YHWH your God — he shall thrust them out from before you and shall drive them out from before you, and you shall possess their land, just as YHWH your God spoke to you.

6. And you shall be very strong to keep and to do all that is written in the book of the Torah of Moses, not turning from it to the right or to the left,

7. so as not to come among these nations, these that remain with you. And the name of their gods you shall not invoke, and you shall not swear by them, and you shall not serve them, and you shall not bow down to them.

8. But to YHWH your God you shall cling, just as you have done to this day.

9. For YHWH has driven out from before you great and mighty nations, and as for you — no man has stood before you to this day.

10. One man of you shall put to flight a thousand, for YHWH your God is the one who fights for you, just as he spoke to you.

11. And you shall guard your souls diligently, to love YHWH your God.

12. For if you turn back at all and cling to the remnant of these nations — these that remain with you — and intermarry with them, and go among them and they among you,

13. know for certain that YHWH your God will not continue to drive out these nations from before you. And they shall become for you a snare and a trap, and a scourge on your sides, and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from upon this good land that YHWH your God has given you.

14. And behold, I am going today the way of all the earth. And you know in all your hearts and in all your souls that not one word has failed from all the good words that YHWH your God spoke concerning you. All have come to pass for you; not one word of them has failed.

15. And it shall be, just as every good word has come upon you that YHWH your God spoke to you, so shall YHWH bring upon you every evil word, until he has destroyed you from upon this good land that YHWH your God has given you —

16. when you transgress the covenant of YHWH your God that he commanded you, and go and serve other gods, and bow down to them. Then the anger of YHWH shall burn against you, and you shall perish quickly from upon the good land that he has given you."

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

- **v1** — Hebrew וַיְהִי מִיָּמִים רַבִּים: "And it was after many days." The temporal phrase מִיָּמִים רַבִּים indicates a significant but unspecified passage of time after the conquest and land allotment.
- **v2** — Joshua summons four categories of leadership: elders (זְקֵנָיו), heads (רָאשָׁיו), judges (שֹׁפְטָיו), and officers (שֹׁטְרָיו). This fourfold structure mirrors the leadership hierarchy established under Moses (cf. Deut 1:15).
- **v4** — הַיַּרְדֵּן rendered "the Jordan" throughout. The territorial description spans from the Jordan eastward to the Mediterranean (הַיָּם הַגָּדוֹל, "the Great Sea") — the full breadth of the promised inheritance.
- **v5** — יֶהְדֹּף (from הדף, "to thrust/push") is a forceful verb: YHWH himself will expel the remaining nations. The doubled promise ("thrust out... drive out") emphasizes divine agency.
- **v6** — "Book of the Torah of Moses" (סֵפֶר תּוֹרַת מֹשֶׁה) — a key phrase linking Joshua's charge to the Deuteronomic instruction (cf. Josh 1:7-8). The idiom "not turning to right or left" signals strict covenant fidelity.
- **v7** — Four prohibited actions regarding foreign gods: invoking their names (תַזְכִּירוּ), swearing by them (תַשְׁבִּיעוּ), serving them (תַעַבְדוּם), and bowing down to them (תִשְׁתַּחֲווּ). The escalating sequence moves from speech to worship.
- **v8** — כִּי אִם בַּיהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם תִּדְבָּקוּ: "But to YHWH your God you shall cling." The verb דבק ("cling/cleave") is the same term used for marital union in Gen 2:24, signifying total covenantal devotion.
- **v11** — "Guard your souls" (וְנִשְׁמַרְתֶּם מְאֹד לְנַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם) — נֶפֶשׁ here denotes the whole self/life, not merely the immaterial "soul." The charge is existential: guard your very lives by loving YHWH.
- **v13** — The fourfold consequence imagery: snare (פַח), trap (מוֹקֵשׁ), scourge on your sides (שֹׁטֵט בְּצִדֵּיכֶם), and thorns in your eyes (צְנִנִים בְּעֵינֵיכֶם). This echoes the warning of Num 33:55.
- **v14** — "The way of all the earth" (בְּדֶרֶךְ כָּל הָאָרֶץ) — a solemn idiom for death, also used by David (1 Kgs 2:2). Joshua's farewell frames mortality as the occasion for covenant urgency.
- **v15** — The symmetry of blessing and curse is emphatic: just as (כַּאֲשֶׁר) every good word came to pass, so (כֵּן) every evil word will come. The covenant is bilateral — YHWH's faithfulness operates in both directions.
- **v16** — The chapter closes with the protasis of covenant violation: transgressing (בְּעָבְרְכֶם), serving (וַעֲבַדְתֶּם), and bowing down (וְהִשְׁתַּחֲוִיתֶם). The consequence — "you shall perish quickly" (וַאֲבַדְתֶּם מְהֵרָה) — mirrors the Deuteronomic curses (Deut 4:26; 11:17).
- **YHWH** — The divine name יהוה is rendered as YHWH throughout, preserving the Tetragrammaton rather than substituting "the LORD."
- **"Sons of Israel"** — Where בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל appears, it is rendered "Sons of Israel" rather than "children of Israel" or "Israelites," maintaining the Hebrew patronymic.
